skill.name: c-pro
skill.description: Write efficient C code with proper memory management, pointer arithmetic, and system calls. Handles embedded systems, kernel modules, and performance-critical code. Use PROACTIVELY for C optimization, memory issues, or system programming.

security_audit.summary: Security review completed. Both static findings are false positives: (1) The backtick syntax on line 28 is markdown code formatting, not shell execution. (2) No cryptographic algorithms exist in the skill content. This is a legitimate C programming assistance skill with no confirmed security risks.

low_findings.title: External Commands Pattern (False Positive)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer detected backtick syntax at line 28, but this is markdown code formatting (resources/implementation-playbook.md), not shell execution. No actual command execution occurs. The backticks are markdown inline code delimiters.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm (False Positive)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged line 3 for weak crypto, but the content is simply a skill description about C programming. No cryptographic code or algorithms are present in this skill.

content.value_statement: Writing safe, efficient C code requires deep expertise in memory management and system programming. This skill provides expert guidance on proper malloc/free patterns, pointer arithmetic, POSIX system calls, and embedded systems development.
actual_capabilities: Generate memory-safe C code with proper malloc/free pairing
Create Makefiles with compiler flags for debugging and optimization
Implement data structures with correct pointer arithmetic
Write POSIX-compliant system call wrappers with error handling
Develop multi-threaded code using pthreads with proper synchronization
Provide guidance on embedded systems programming and resource constraints
limitations: Does not compile or execute code directly
Does not provide IDE integration or debugging sessions
Does not generate assembly code or machine-specific optimizations
Cannot verify code against specific hardware constraints without detailed specifications

use_cases.title: Systems Programming Projects
use_cases.description: Get expert guidance on writing system-level C code with proper memory management, system calls, and error handling for operating systems or driver development.
use_cases.target_user: Systems programmers building OS components or drivers

use_cases.title: Embedded Development
use_cases.description: Receive assistance with resource-constrained embedded systems programming, including memory pool allocation, stack optimization, and hardware interaction patterns.
use_cases.target_user: Embedded software engineers working with microcontrollers

use_cases.title: Code Review and Optimization
use_cases.description: Get detailed review of existing C code with valgrind-clean output, performance benchmarking suggestions, and clang-tidy improvements.
use_cases.target_user: Developers seeking to improve existing C codebase quality

prompt_templates.title: Basic C Code Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a C function that [describe the task]. Include proper error handling and memory management.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need a simple C function with proper resource handling

prompt_templates.title: Memory-Safe Data Structure
prompt_templates.prompt: Implement a [data structure name] in C with proper memory allocation, deallocation, and boundary checking. Use C99 standard.
prompt_templates.scenario: When implementing custom data structures that need leak-free memory management

prompt_templates.title: POSIX System Call Wrapper
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a safe wrapper around [system call name] that handles all error conditions, sets errno correctly, and includes proper documentation.
prompt_templates.scenario: When wrapping system calls for robust error handling

prompt_templates.title: Multi-threaded C Application
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a multi-threaded C program using pthreads that [describe functionality]. Include mutex locks for shared resources and proper thread cleanup.
prompt_templates.scenario: When developing concurrent C applications with thread synchronization

output_examples.input: Write a function to parse a CSV file and return an array of strings
output: A C function that opens the file with fopen, allocates memory for each line using malloc, properly handles fgetsc errors, and includes a cleanup function to free all allocated memory. The function returns NULL on failure with errno set appropriately.

output_examples.input: How do I implement a thread-safe counter in C?
output: A pthreads-based implementation showing a mutex-protected counter structure, with initialization, increment with lock/unlock, and destruction functions. Includes example usage demonstrating proper lock acquisition and release patterns.

best_practices: Always check return values from malloc and system calls - never assume allocation or system operations succeed
Use static analysis tools like clang-tidy and valgrind regularly to catch memory leaks and undefined behavior
Follow C99/C11 standards and use compiler warnings (-Wall -Wextra -Werror) to catch potential issues at compile time
anti_patterns: Not checking malloc return value - a NULL return causes undefined behavior when dereferenced
Using printf format specifiers incorrectly - mismatched types cause undefined behavior and security vulnerabilities
Forgetting to include include guards in headers - causes linker errors with multiple inclusions

faq.question: Does this skill compile code for me?
faq.answer: No, this skill provides guidance and generates code patterns. You will need to compile the code yourself using GCC, Clang, or another C compiler.

faq.question: Which C standard does this skill use?
faq.answer: The skill focuses on C99 and C11 standards, as these provide modern features like designated initializers and improved type generics.

faq.question: Can this help with embedded systems programming?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill includes guidance on embedded systems development, including memory pool allocation, stack usage minimization, and hardware interaction patterns.

faq.question: Does this skill support C++?
faq.answer: No, this skill focuses specifically on C programming. For C++ assistance, a separate C++ skill would be more appropriate.

faq.question: How does this skill handle memory leaks?
faq.answer: The skill emphasizes proper malloc/free pairing, provides cleanup functions, and recommends using valgrind to verify memory is properly freed.

faq.question: Can this help with debugging?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill provides guidance on using GDB and Valgrind for debugging memory issues, undefined behavior, and performance problems.
